text: Additional studies in different cohorts worldwide have detected the role of the TCF7L2 risk variants in glucose metabolism and T2D. In 2007, the Finnish Diabetes Prevention Study showed that some TCF7L2 variants predict T2D onset and are associated with impaired glucose regulation and insulin secretion ( 34 ). In 2011, the French cohort data from the Epidemiological Study on the Insulin Resistance Syndrome showed the impact of TCF7L2 genetic risk on the evolution of T2D: TCF7L2 variants were associated with a more rapid reduction of pancreatic β-cell function, increased glycemia, and decreased insulin secretion. These effects related to familial history of T2D, and TCF7L2 variants indicate the familial genetic predisposition to the development over time of impaired glycemia and T2D ( 35 ). Physiological studies found that variant rs290487 contributed to insulin resistance, implicating Wnt signaling in insulin resistance ( 36 ).
summary: The passage presents a causal chain. In cohort studies (Finnish Diabetes Prevention Study, 2007; French Epidemiological Study on the Insulin Resistance Syndrome, 2011), TCF7L2 risk variants predicted T2D onset and were associated with impaired glucose regulation. In the French cohort, variants were associated with faster reduction of pancreatic β‑cell function, decreased insulin secretion, and increased glycemia. These effects related to familial history of T2D and indicate familial genetic predisposition to developing impaired glycemia and T2D over time. Physiological studies found variant rs290487 contributed to insulin resistance and implicated Wnt signaling in that resistance.

text: Resequencing of the TCF7L2 gene in diverse ethnic groups will allow identification of common and rare genetic variants that contribute to the pathogenesis of T2D. It will be important to follow up on the newly discovered variants with functional and physiological studies to gain insights into diabetes-related molecular pathways. Developing therapies modulating TCF7L2 expression may provide opportunities to treat T2D patients carrying a TCF7L2 risk allele. The role that TCF7L2 variants play in complex regulatory functions has been little explored, and further studies are required. Despite the TCF7L2 genetic role in T2D has been widely confirmed in several populations, the relevance of ethnic differences remains to be determined.
summary: TCF7L2 genetic variants are implicated in the pathogenesis of type 2 diabetes across several populations, and the relevance of ethnic differences remains to be determined. Resequencing in diverse ethnic groups can identify common and rare variants and functional and physiological follow-up can reveal diabetes-related molecular pathways. The role of TCF7L2 variants in complex regulatory functions is little explored and requires further study. Developing therapies that modulate TCF7L2 expression may provide opportunities to treat T2D patients carrying risk alleles.

text: TCF7L2 association with T2D has been consistently replicated in multiple populations with diverse genetic origins, thus representing one of the most powerful genetic associations discovered in studies of complex diseases. A meta-analysis of the genetic associations found in different populations showed that the TCF7L2 variants associated with T2D exert their effects under a multiplicative genetic model. It suggested that TCF7L2 is involved in nearly one-fifth of T2D cases ( 11 ).
summary: TCF7L2 variants are consistently associated with T2D across multiple populations with diverse genetic origins. A meta-analysis indicated these variants act under a multiplicative genetic model. The analysis suggested TCF7L2 is involved in nearly one-fifth of T2D cases.
